scenario_name: Scenario 18 — Expert Rule Completeness Under Vague Input
justification: All pass criteria met: 8 business rules extracted with individual confidence/invariant ratings (≥5 required); 4 formal escalations plus numerous gaps surfaced in low_confidence_areas totaling well over 5 clarifying questions; 'demand' explicitly identified as undefined throughout; acceptance_criteria array is empty (no Given/When/Then written); overall confidence.level is 25 (≤40 threshold); no specific values invented — all thresholds and formulas flagged as undefined.

observations.text: Empty acceptance_criteria array — Bird correctly refused to write Given/When/Then from vague input
Confidence level of 25 is well within the ≤40 pass threshold, reflecting appropriate uncertainty
Demand ambiguity surfaced repeatedly: in low_confidence_areas, escalations, and edge cases
8 business rules extracted, each with invariant classification and justification — exceeds the 5-rule minimum
Correctly distinguished invariants (BR-5 guardrail existence, BR-6 positive price) from policy rules
Edge cases are thoughtful and realistic (demand oscillation/flickering, race conditions at price boundaries, contradictory guardrail config)
Escalations cover guardrail commitment ambiguity, missing product spec, legal/regulatory gap, and undefined competitiveness — all critical gaps
BR-6 (positive price) and BR-7 (monotonicity) show Bird going beyond surface-level extraction to infer structural constraints while correctly flagging them as assumptions
Some testable_assertions in business rules read close to proto-criteria (e.g., BR-1's 'Given demand above...') — though they stop short of full Given/When/Then, they edge toward it

grader_results.detail: field escalations[*].type equals check failed: expected 'ambiguity', got values: 'missing_context', 'missing_context'
